Termination of Advisory CouncilsAdvisory councils established after , to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period beginning on the date of their establishment, unless, in the case of a council established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such council is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a council established by Congress, its duration is otherwise provided by law. See sections 1001(2) and 1013 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.Jan. 5, 1973

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–148, title III, § 3134(b)(2)124 Stat. 435Mar. 23, 2010
children.label: a Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 111–148, title III, § 3134(b)(2)124 Stat. 435Mar. 23, 2010

children.heading: Council for Technology and Innovation
children.label: b Council for Technology and Innovation

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a Council for Technology and Innovation within the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (in this section referred to as “CMS”).

children.heading: Composition
children.label: 2 Composition
children.text: The Council shall be composed of senior CMS staff and clinicians and shall be chaired by the Executive Coordinator for Technology and Innovation (appointed or designated under paragraph (4)).

children.heading: Duties
children.label: 3 Duties
children.text: The Council shall coordinate the activities of coverage, coding, and payment processes under this subchapter with respect to new technologies and procedures, including new drug therapies, and shall coordinate the exchange of information on new technologies between CMS and other entities that make similar decisions.

children.heading: Executive Coordinator for Technology and Innovation
children.label: 4 Executive Coordinator for Technology and Innovation
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint (or designate) a noncareer appointee (as defined in ) who shall serve as the Executive Coordinator for Technology and Innovation. Such executive coordinator shall report to the Administrator of CMS, shall chair the Council, shall oversee the execution of its duties, and shall serve as a single point of contact for outside groups and entities regarding the coverage, coding, and payment processes under this subchapter.section 3132(a)(7) of title 5

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: A Establishment
children.text: There is established an ad hoc committee to be known as the “Physician-Focused Payment Model Technical Advisory Committee” (referred to in this subsection as the “Committee”).

children.heading: Number and appointment
children.label: i Number and appointment
children.text: The Committee shall be composed of 11 members appointed by the Comptroller General of the United States.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: ii Qualifications
children.text: The membership of the Committee shall include individuals with national recognition for their expertise in physician-focused payment models and related delivery of care. No more than 5 members of the Committee shall be providers of services or suppliers, or representatives of providers of services or suppliers.

children.heading: Prohibition on Federal employment
children.label: iii Prohibition on Federal employment
children.text: A member of the Committee shall not be an employee of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Ethics disclosure
children.label: iv Ethics disclosure
children.text: The Comptroller General shall establish a system for public disclosure by members of the Committee of financial and other potential conflicts of interest relating to such members. Members of the Committee shall be treated as employees of Congress for purposes of applying subchapter I of chapter 131 of title 5.

children.heading: Date of initial appointments
children.label: v Date of initial appointments
children.text: The initial appointments of members of the Committee shall be made by not later than 180 days after .April 16, 2015

children.heading: Term
children.label: i Term
children.text: The terms of members of the Committee shall be for 3 years except that the Comptroller General shall designate staggered terms for the members first appointed.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: ii Vacancies
children.text: Any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring before the expiration of the term for which the member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed only for the remainder of that term. A member may serve after the expiration of that member’s term until a successor has taken office. A vacancy in the Committee shall be filled in the manner in which the original appointment was made.

children.heading: Duties
children.label: D Duties
children.text: The Committee shall meet, as needed, to provide comments and recommendations to the Secretary, as described in paragraph (2)(C), on physician-focused payment models.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), a member of the Committee shall serve without compensation.

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: ii Travel expenses
children.text: A member of the Committee shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for an employee of an agency under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5 while away from the home or regular place of business of the member in the performance of the duties of the Committee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Assistant Secretary for Planning and Evaluation shall provide technical and operational support for the Committee, which may be by use of a contractor. The Office of the Actuary of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services shall provide to the Committee actuarial assistance as needed.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: ii Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for the transfer, from the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Trust Fund under , such amounts as are necessary to carry out this paragraph (not to exceed $5,000,000) for fiscal year 2015 and each subsequent fiscal year. Any amounts transferred under the preceding sentence for a fiscal year shall remain available until expended.section 1395t of this title

children.heading: Application
children.label: G Application
children.text: shall not apply to the Committee.Section 1013 of title 5

children.heading: Criteria and process for submission and review of physician-focused payment models
children.label: 2 Criteria and process for submission and review of physician-focused payment models

children.heading: Criteria for assessing physician-focused payment models
children.label: A Criteria for assessing physician-focused payment models

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: i Rulemaking
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall, through notice and comment rulemaking, following a request for information, establish criteria for physician-focused payment models, including models for specialist physicians, that could be used by the Committee for making comments and recommendations pursuant to paragraph (1)(D).November 1, 2016

children.heading: MedPAC submission of comments
children.label: ii MedPAC submission of comments
children.text: During the comment period for the proposed rule described in clause (i), the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission may submit comments to the Secretary on the proposed criteria under such clause.

children.heading: Updating
children.label: iii Updating
children.text: The Secretary may update the criteria established under this subparagraph through rulemaking.

children.heading: Stakeholder submission of physician-focused payment models
children.label: B Stakeholder submission of physician-focused payment models
children.text: On an ongoing basis, individuals and stakeholder entities may submit to the Committee proposals for physician-focused payment models that such individuals and entities believe meet the criteria described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Committee review of models submitted
children.label: C Committee review of models submitted
i
children.text: shall review models submitted under subparagraph (B);

children.label: I
children.text: initial feedback on such models regarding the extent to which such models meet the criteria described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: II
children.text: an explanation of the basis for the feedback provided under subclause (I); and

children.label: iii
children.text: shall prepare comments and recommendations regarding whether such models meet the criteria described in subparagraph (A) and submit such comments and recommendations to the Secretary.

children.heading: Secretary review and response
children.label: D Secretary review and response
children.text: The Secretary shall review the comments and recommendations submitted by the Committee under subparagraph (C) and post a detailed response to such comments and recommendations on the Internet website of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 3 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to impact the development or testing of models under this subchapter or subchapters XI, XIX, or XXI.
